title: Kazdan Paratus
content:

{{Quote|Don't worry, Masters. I'll defend you from this Sith trash!|Kazdan Paratus, to inanimate trash replicas of the Jedi Council, before fighting Galen Marek|<ref name="TFUvg" />}}
'''Kazdan Paratus''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Aleena/Legends|Aleena]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] who served the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] as a [[Jedi General/Legends|General]] of the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. When the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] came to power and the Republic and Jedi Order were dissolved, Paratus abandoned his colleagues and fled to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Raxus Prime/Legends|Raxus Prime]] on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Living in isolation on the junk planet, Paratus slowly descended into madness, attempting to reconstruct the Order from the junk that surrounded him. He was [[First mission to Raxus Prime|killed]] by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader's]] secret apprentice [[Galen Marek]] [[3 BBY/Legends|sixteen years]] after the Clone Wars ended, and Raxus Prime was subsequently annexed by the Empire.

==Biography==
===Early life===
[[File:Kazd.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Kazdan Paratus.]]
A [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Aleena/Legends|Aleena]], Kazdan Paratus was taken to the [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] for [[Jedi training/Legends|training]] in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. During his time at the [[Jedi academy|Temple's academy]], Paratus lacked the martial skills of his peers, and instead focused on building machines and tinkering with [[Droid/Legends|droids]]; his lack of social skills troubled his [[Jedi instructor|instructors]] and peers. Following an apprenticeship with a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]], Paratus surprised his mentors when he successfully passed his [[Jedi Trials/Legends|Trials of Knighthood]] through unusual means. Constructing [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] to assist in the Trials, he passed and was given the rank of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] by the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]].<ref name="TFUvg" />

Preferring to remain at the Temple during his tenure as a Knight, Paratus did take the occasional mission, using his battle droids to assist him when conflict arose. He was stationed at the Temple as an instructor at the academy and worked as a member of the [[Technical division/Legends|Technical division]], crafting [[Jedi training droid/Legends|training droids]] for the Temple. During this time, he constructed a [[Lightsaber pike/Legends|lightsaber pike]] with a [[blue]] blade.<ref name="TFUvg" /> He eventually became the official [[engineer/Legends|engineer]] of the Temple, keeping a workshop on the capital planet.<ref name="TFU novel" />

===The Clone Wars===
When the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] erupted between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], Paratus could not refuse the opportunity to study the droids of the Confederacy. Paratus served as a [[Jedi General/Legends|General]] in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]], positioning himself on the front lines, also building [[Medical droid/Legends|medical]] and [[Power droid/Legends|power droids]] as well as other machines designed to support the Grand Army. To increase his size and mobility, he designed four mechanical arms to wear on his back. Eventually, he took command of a group of [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] that were mostly killed off in a disastrous campaign. Due to these circumstances, he was forced to create his own troops that consisted of combat droids that he assembled using makeshift parts. After a great battle, Paratus returned to the Temple and continued teaching at the academy.<ref name="TFUvg" /><ref name="TFU novel" />

It was possibly around this time that Paratus was made a Jedi Master.<ref name="TFU novel">[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed (novelization)|''Star Wars: The Force Unleashed'' novelization]]</ref>

===Great Jedi Purge===
{{Quote|Kazdan Paratus is far more powerful than you. I do not expect you to survive. But if you destroy him, you'll be one step closer to your destiny.|Darth Vader, to Galen Marek|<ref name="TFUvg" />}}
Stationed at the Temple in the final days of the war, Paratus was present following the issuance of [[Contingency Orders for the Grand Army of the Republic: Order Initiation, Orders 1 Through 150|Contingency]] [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]] by the [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]]. The order, effectively disbanding the Jedi Order, was a plan by the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]] to trap and destroy the Jedi. When [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] led the [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]] [[Operation: Knightfall/Legends|against]] the Temple, Paratus suffered a breakdown and fled in terror from the Temple, leaving his peers and his [[Jedi Initiate/Legends|students]] to die.<ref name="TFUvg" /> He escaped Coruscant in a nondescript [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] with several droid prototypes in tow.<ref name="Star Wars: The Force Unleashed: Prima Official Game Guide">[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars: The Force Unleashed'': Prima Official Game Guide]]</ref>

Wracked with guilt over his abandonment of the Jedi, Paratus descended into insanity. Going into hiding on the waste-covered [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Raxus Prime/Legends|Raxus Prime]], he became a reclusive droid maker. Paratus built an army of personally customized [[Scrap Guardian|combat droids]] to protect himself from intruders, creating [[Junk Titan|massive droids]] and [[Junk golem|sentries]] held together by the Force. In his attempt to rebuild the Jedi Order, Paratus built a [[Junk Temple|replica]] of the Coruscant Jedi Temple out of refuse and made puppets of all the High Councilors for the Council Chamber, arming them with various melee weapons. In his delusional state, Paratus would hold mock Council meetings, vowing to protect them from harm.<ref name="TFUvg" />

===Duel and death===
{{Quote|You are not welcome here!|Kazdan Paratus to Starkiller|<ref name="TFUvg" />}}
[[File:Paratus_vs_Starkiller.jpg|thumb|right|201px|Kazdan Paratus duels Starkiller.]]
Approximately [[3 BBY/Legends|16 years]] after the collapse of the Jedi Order, the insane Jedi was discovered by Darth Vader who sent his secret [[Sith apprentice/Legends|assassin]], [[Galen Marek|Starkiller]], on a mission to eliminate the Aleena Jedi. Paratus noticed his ship landing and consulted a metallic effigy of [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] as to this matter and grew nervous after his silent "answer," moving his lightsaber from hand to hand.<ref name="TFU novel" />

After navigating the junk world, the assassin arrived in Paratus's facsimile Temple and was [[First mission to Raxus Prime|confronted]] by a [[Junk Titan|Titan]] armed with two large [[Vibro-ax/Legends|vibro-axes]] and a variety of weapons tipping its other arms. The Sith apprentice dodged blows from the junk warrior, and as the floor buckled under the Titan's weight, knocked off one of its weapons with a [[Telekinesis/Legends|telekinetic punch]]. Dodging another attack by the golem, slicing as he went, Starkiller removed more of its weapons. He rolled through the machine's legs and cut up its spine, scoring a deep blow.<ref name="TFUvg" />

The crippled machine attempted to turn at the man, but he used his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] to cut at both its vibro-axe arms, forcing it to drop one axe. He sent a barrage of lightning at the machine, sending all the strength he could muster at it. Its [[Photoreceptor/Legends|photoreceptors]] became nonfunctional, though it still charged at Marek with its other axe. The apprentice used the Force to push the creature, knocking it off one of its legs through the walls of the Temple, following it and finally bringing it down in a junk version of the [[Jedi Council Chamber/Legends|High Council Chamber]] of the Jedi Temple with junk effigies of the late or missing Council members.<ref name="TFU novel" />

[[File:ParatusDeath.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The death of Kazdan Paratus.]]

From a hatch in the machine's front emerged the Jedi Master that Starkiller had been searching for. Paratus swore he would protect the "Masters" and the mannequins began to stir, distracting the apprentice. Paratus lunged at the invader, [[Shiim/Legends|slightly scoring]] a cut down Starkiller's left arm before it was parried. Though the assassin fought back, every move he made was either blocked almost instantly by the Aleena's [[Lightsaber pike/Legends|lightsaber pike]] or missed because of the Jedi's constant hopping from place to place. During the battle, Starkiller was suddenly struck from behind and his lightsaber [[Sun djem/Legends|fell from his hand]].<ref name="TFU novel" />

Confronted by a replica of Master [[Plo Koon/Legends|Plo Koon]] standing behind him in a [[Form V/Legends|Form V]] stance, a [[Vibroblade/Legends|vibroblade]] in place of his lightsaber. After a moment of surprise, Starkiller used the Force to blow apart the patchwork tribute and regained his lightsaber, deflecting a blow from the recovered Paratus. More "Masters", including [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], Yoda, and Anakin Skywalker attempted to face the apprentice, but all were destroyed. With each "death," Paratus clasped his chest deeper in pain and despair at their loss, crying out when the last one, Master [[Agen Kolar/Legends|Agen Kolar]], fell.<ref name="TFU novel" />

The old Jedi then attempted a telekinetic assault, but the Sith apprentice fought back, catching the massive chunk of machinery Paratus used and throwing it back. Paratus dodged, leaping onto the narrow window struts above the chamber floor, with Marek in pursuit. Starkiller, worried that he might experience another vision as he had while dueling [[Rahm Kota/Legends|Rahm Kota]], intensified his attack, battering Paratus with debris and detritus. The two exchanged blows on the ledge, until Paratus suddenly retreated and fired a blast of Force energy at the dark sider. Starkiller blocked and rebounded it, slamming Paratus into one of the support struts. The Aleena retreated to the chamber floor and again attempted a telekinetic assault, casting another massive chunk of machinery at the assassin. However, Starkiller again caught it and threw it back, sending it crashing into the Jedi Master, crushing him against the chamber floor and mortally wounding him.<ref name="TFUvg" />

As he lay dying, Paratus uttered his last words, apologizing to the 'Masters' for failing them again. Paratus then became one with the Force and his body disappeared in a flash. Starkiller took Paratus' lightsaber and left the ruined temple.<ref name="TFU novel" />

===Legacy===
{{Quote|I'm sorry, masters. I've failed you… again.|Kazdan Paratus' last words|<ref name="TFUvg" />}}
After Paratus' death, Starkiller claimed Paratus's lightsaber pike as a gift for his master, as other than being snapped in half, it had survived the ordeal it had gone through when Paratus was crushed. Although he took the damaged weapon to his quarters and attempted for an hour to repair it, he was unsuccessful in restoring the weapon as he could not realign the [[Lightsaber crystal/Legends|lightsaber crystals]] to the lens and failed to connect the [[Emitter matrix/Legends|emitter matrix]] to the power conduit. After failing to fix it in meditation, it was discarded by Starkiller.<ref name="TFU novel" />

Later, [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]] learned about Darth Vader's secret apprentice and their plan to kill him. Palpatine had his spies follow the ''[[Rogue Shadow]]'' to the [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']], Darth Vader's still uncompleted [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]]. The Emperor boarded and told Vader to kill the apprentice, at which he reluctantly complied, [[Shiak|impaling]] Starkiller, using the Force to throw him around the room, and finally out of a [[Viewport/Legends|viewport]] into the vacuum of space. While he floated there, drifting in and out of consciousness, he experienced numerous visions of past and future events, including one of Paratus worriedly watching the ''Rogue Shadow'' land on the surface of Raxus Prime.<ref name="TFU novel" />

After Paratus' death, Raxus Prime was left defenseless, as Paratus' droids had protected the planet during his lifetime. [[Drexl Roosh]] was angered by Paratus' death as the droids kept the Empire at bay for many years. Although [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] scavengers remained on the planet for some time, the Empire soon took over the planet, stripping its metals to create [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]], leaving little for scavengers to obtain. The metals and ores taken from the planet after Paratus' death were used in orbiting construction facilities, such as the one Starkiller later destroyed using an [[ore cannon]].<ref name="TFUvg" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Our home… Stop ruining our home!|Kazdan Paratus' insane ranting as he duels Galen Marek|<ref name="TFUvg" />}}
As a young Jedi, Paratus was more antisocial than some Padawans, preferring to hole himself up in his personal quarters to work upon his droids than to socialize. Not possessing the detachment of most Jedi, Paratus created the droids originally to aid with training Padawans in the Temple, and managed to pass his trials not through physical prowess with a lightsaber, but with intelligence. However, when Order 66 was initiated, and Operation: Knightfall subsequently occurred, Paratus fled the Jedi Temple in an act of his own perception of cowardice, leaving his comrades to their fate at the hands of Darth Vader and the 501st Legion.<ref name="TFUvg" />

After abandoning the Jedi at the Temple on Coruscant, Paratus was consumed by guilt, which would eventually lead to madness. The initial stages of his insanity caused Paratus to form an inferiority complex regarding his small size, prompting him to construct the four mechanical limbs he kept strapped to his back to provide him with the desired height and mobility.<ref name="TFU CG">''[[The Force Unleashed Campaign Guide]]''</ref> The next stage of his madness caused him to fall into a delusion of being a member of the Jedi Council, which in turn motivated him to construct the replica of the Jedi Temple out of the abundant debris on Raxus Prime.<ref name="TFU novel" /> By the time Paratus faced Marek in combat, the Aleena Jedi was past the brink of [[schizophrenia]], as shown by how he reconstructed all the Jedi Council members out of junk and believing them to be the actual individuals.<ref name="TFUvg" />

==Powers and abilities==
{{Quote|I realized power, I realized fate, I realized the Dark Side.|Paratus to Galen Marek|<ref name="TFUvg" />}}
Paratus was extremely talented in cybernetics, and created several ''ad hoc'' unique machines far more advanced than the existing droid technology.<ref name="TFU novel" />
[[File:Paratus_saber.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Kazdan Paratus's lightsaber pike]]
Due to a special robotic rig on his back which served as additional limbs for him, Paratus was able to walk on walls and vertical surfaces.<ref name="TFUvg" /> A [[Form V/Legends|Shien]] practitioner,<ref name="TFU CG" /> Paratus handled [[Kazdan Paratus's lightsaber pike|his lightsaber pike]] with two of his mechanical limbs to move around in haste, his lightsaber pike was the requirement for his mechanical arms to use with deadly precision and wield his chosen weapon with great dexterity, as shown by the way he mounted almost continuous attacks upon Starkiller during their duel. He was also able to use his lightsaber staff to block the various counter-attacks utilized by Starkiller.<ref name="TFUvg" /> He appeared to know the lightsaber form of every member of the Jedi Council who was present by the end of the Clone Wars; he used that intimate knowledge via the Force to imbue on the mannequins of those Council members, causing them to mimic each individual fighting style.<ref name="TFU novel" />

Adept with the Force, Paratus had a great level of control over his droid creations, and was [[Technometry|capable of giving life to robotic creations that were subject to his will]]. He was capable of making such creations like the Junk Titan in a short span of time and with ease. With his death, all of the junk creatures he created collapsed. In combat, Paratus had the ability to suspend himself in mid-air and to hurl objects at an opponent using the Force, as he demonstrated against Starkiller.<ref name="TFUvg" />

==Behind the scenes==
Kazdan Paratus was voiced by [[Larry Drake]] in [[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed|''The Force Unleashed'' video game]].

===Conception===
[[File:KazdanParatusModelandConcept.jpg|thumb|left|Kazdan Paratus untextured model and concept art.]]
Kazdan was conceived in order to show a more technologically-oriented character, and environment, contrasting the scenery of the [[Mission to Felucia (Great Jedi Purge)|Shaak Ti mission]] and [[Felucia/Legends|Felucia]]. Initially, Kazdan was envisioned as a brooding humanoid, like [[Rahm Kota/Legends|Rahm Kota]] and [[Shaak Ti/Legends|Shaak Ti]], Starkiller's other targets; but after seeing concept art from [[Krome Studios]], the developer team decided to make him an exotic-looking alien and differentiate him.<ref name="art" />

The cybernetic appendages were thought to make gameplay more interesting, as they would allow him to increase his stature, and even climb walls during the duel. Their in-story purpose was to help him build droids faster.<ref name="art" />

An early biography of Kazdan described him as an accomplished warrior, who, however, preferred to avoid battle. During the Clone Wars he fought in the Outer Rim, until his clone contigent was wiped out soon. Left alone, he used Separatist droid scraps to construct his own army, which ensured his survival during the Great Jedi Purge. From there, finding his way to Raxus Prime, he continued building droid body guards to fend off Darth Vader.<ref name="art">''[[The Art and Making of Star Wars: The Force Unleashed]]''</ref>

===Differences between versions===
Kazdan Paratus wields a blue [[Lightsaber pike/Legends|lightsaber pike]] in the [[Wikipedia:Xbox 360|Xbox 360]] and [[Wikipedia:PlayStation 3|PlayStation 3]] versions of the game, but a yellow lightsaber pike in the [[Wikipedia:Nintendo DS|Nintendo DS]] version, and a yellow [[Double-bladed lightsaber/Legends|double-bladed lightsaber]] with an elongated hilt in the [[Wikipedia:Wii|Wii]], [[Wikipedia:PlayStation 2|PlayStation 2]], and [[Wikipedia:PlayStation Portable|PlayStation Portable]] versions. The novelization also describes his lightsaber staff as having two blades, one significantly shorter than the other. In ''[[The Force Unleashed Campaign Guide]]'', Paratus is depicted as having a yellow Long-handle lightsaber.<ref name="TFU CG" />

Paratus' death also plays out differently in the Wii and PS2 versions; instead of crushing him with junk as in the PS3 and Xbox 360 version, Starkiller smashes him against a wall before finishing him off with [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]].<ref name="TFUvg" />

Kazdan Paratus does not appear in the [[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed (graphic novel)|graphic novel]] adaptation of The Force Unleashed; Vader sends Starkiller directly to Felucia right after the duel with Rahm Kota.<ref name="Star Wars: The Force Unleashed comic">[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed (graphic novel)|''Star Wars: The Force Unleashed'' graphic novel]]</ref>

For some reason [[Shaak Ti/Legends|Shaak Ti]] isn't among Kazdan's junk Jedi Council in the [[Wikipedia:PlayStation 3|PlayStation 3]] version of the game. This may be a reference to the [[Aleena/Legends|Aleena]] Jedi Master [[Tsui Choi/Legends|Tsui Choi]], who was supposed to take Shaak Ti's seat on the Council before it was decided that she wouldn't die at the hands of [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]].

The [[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed (novelization)|novelization]] gives a different story, that he survived Order 66 while being in the front, protected by his droid bodyguards. ''[[The Art and Making of Star Wars: The Force Unleashed]]'' gives a similar backstory. This article presents the backstory given in the in-game databank, according to which Kazdan was in the Temple during the issue of Order 66, from which he escaped.
